heaviest_rainfall.status: The storm today is ranked highest for the amount of rainfall recorded during one storm.  40.92mm fell at a maximum rate of 2.2mm per hour over 122.6 hours.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 27.0mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: A storm today ranked top for the longest continuous rainfall according to our records, lasting a total of122.6 hours.  It came with 40.92mm of rainfall, at times, falling at 2.2mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: An active storm has been detected in the vicinity.  The last storm experienced was on 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: It is currently one of the longest rainy periods.  It is the ranked 9th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 8 consecutive days where it has been rainy starting on 07 November 2025.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (186°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 13 October 2012.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 5%) and rainfall (up 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 351 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 439 m), rainfall (down 0.1 mm), feels like temperature (down 2.0°C), and outside temperature (down 2.3°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 351.0m lower than yesterday, 216.8m lower than the past week, 438.9m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 637m to a maximum of 1658m, averaging 602.4m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 819.3m and 1041.3m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, air moisture should feel ok and light winds will prevail. Chance of light rain, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and light levels will be low during the day. Low pressure may bring unsettled weather.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (95%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
